edges.description: AR variants impair receptor expression, ligand binding, DNA binding, receptor interactions, or downstream transcriptional activation.
Reduced receptor signaling makes androgen-responsive tissues only partially responsive to age-appropriate androgen exposure.
Partial androgen resistance produces a spectrum of hypospadias, micropenis, bifid or fused labioscrotal structures, and ambiguous genitalia.
Partial androgen resistance can alter pubertal sex-steroid balance and breast development.
Partial androgen resistance can reduce or otherwise alter androgen-dependent secondary sexual hair.
Reduced androgen receptor signaling in testicular somatic cells disrupts spermatogenesis and contributes to infertility and sexual dysfunction.
Partial androgen resistance is associated with cryptorchidism and abnormal scrotal development.
Partial androgen target-tissue resistance alters hypothalamic-pituitary- gonadal feedback while testosterone and Sertoli-cell hormone production are preserved.
intermediate_mechanisms: impaired androgen negative feedback with preserved gonadal hormone production

edges.description: Partial androgen resistance can reduce pubertal voice masculinization, producing an abnormally high-pitched voice.
Impaired androgen feedback is associated with elevated circulating luteinizing hormone.
Gonadal testosterone production is preserved or increased relative to the androgen-resistance phenotype.
Preserved gonadal androgen production with aromatization can alter circulating estrogen levels.
intermediate_mechanisms: aromatization of preserved gonadal androgen production

edges.description: Estradiol may be increased downstream of preserved androgen production and peripheral aromatization.
intermediate_mechanisms: aromatization of preserved gonadal androgen production

edges.description: Preserved Sertoli-cell function in the 46,XY DSD context is associated with increased antimullerian hormone.
Partial androgen-dependent urethral and external-genital development produces hypospadias.
More severe undervirilization can place the urethral opening in the perineal position.
Reduced androgen responsiveness during genital development can produce micropenis.
Variable residual androgen action produces ambiguous external genitalia.
Incomplete labioscrotal fusion during androgen-dependent development can produce a bifid scrotum.
Variable genital virilization can present as clitoral hypertrophy in predominantly female external genital presentations.
Partial labioscrotal fusion in a predominantly female phenotype can manifest as fused labia majora.
Abnormal androgen-dependent urogenital development can include a urogenital sinus anomaly.
Mullerian regression in the 46,XY PAIS context commonly leaves the uterus absent.
Normal 46,XY testis development means ovarian tissue is absent.
Regression or absence of upper Mullerian-derived structures can produce a blind-ending vagina.
Uterine aplasia and absent Mullerian-derived reproductive structures can present as primary amenorrhea.
Pubertal androgen resistance can produce abnormal androgen-dependent secondary sexual hair.
Impaired spermatogenesis downstream of partial AR dysfunction can produce azoospermia.
Partial AR dysfunction impairs spermatogenesis and is associated with male infertility.
Partial androgen resistance can impair male sexual function.
Abnormal testicular descent manifests as bilateral cryptorchidism.
Retained undescended gonadal tissue creates a tumor-risk management issue in AIS and is consistent with Orphanet's germ-cell-neoplasia listing for PAIS.
Retained gonadal tissue in PAIS carries a recognized risk of germ cell neoplasia.
